title: 10 best herbal teas for gum swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with gum swelling because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce inflammation in the gums. The flavonoids present in sage tea also help to strengthen the blood vessels, which can become damaged due to poor oral hygiene or other factors, leading to gum swelling. Additionally, sage tea's antiseptic properties help to combat bacteria that can cause gum infections and further contribute to swelling.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Sage tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum swelling by neutralizing free radicals and promoting healthy gum tissue.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce gum swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.

Antimicrobial Properties: Sage tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing symptoms of gingivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, contains sesquiterpenes, which are known to reduce inflammation and promote healing in gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in quantities of one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Combine the harvested sage leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose herb particles.
Add honey to taste and stir well to dissolve, using a maximum of one tablespoon per cup.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation while still warm, ideally 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from the plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can help reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms associated with swollen gums, such as pain, redness, and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory effects of purple coneflower teas make it an effective natural remedy for gum swelling, providing relief and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosinic acid, which reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, helping to alleviate swelling.
Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Antioxidant activity: Purple coneflower tea's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that damage gum tissue, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can lead to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce gum inflammation and swelling.
Barks: Contain tannins that possess astringent properties, making them effective in reducing gum bleeding and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers or use dried flower petals in the amount of 2-3 teaspoons per cup of water.
Boil filtered water and pour it over the herb mixture in a heat-resistant teapot or mug for 5-7 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep undisturbed for an additional 10-15 minutes to release maximum amounts of active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately and consume at least three cups a day for optimal oral health benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort. The antioxidants present in ginger also work to soothe and calm irritated gums, reducing inflammation and swelling. Additionally, the warm and soothing nature of ginger tea can help to ease any tension or stress that may be contributing to gum swelling. By sipping on a cup of herbal ginger tea, you can experience relief from swollen and irritated gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable smile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissues and contribute to swelling, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing oxidative stress.

Analgesic and pain-relieving compounds: Ginger contains analgesic compounds like gingerol and salicylate, which help reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum inflammation and swelling, making it a natural remedy for soothing sore gums.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Roots: Contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids that have antimicrobial properties, making them effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root and peel it to remove the skin.
Grind 1-2 inches of peeled ginger into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ginger with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the ginger mixture for 5-7 minutes before straining and discarding the solids.
Mix the infused liquid with honey to taste and consume immediately after cooling down.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, reduces prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause inflammation and swelling. When consumed as an herbal tea, curcumin is absorbed quickly into the bloodstream and transported to the gums, where it helps to alleviate swollen and inflamed tissues. Additionally, turmeric's antimicrobial properties help combat gum infections, further reducing swelling and promoting healthy oral tissue. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from gum swelling and related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidants: Turmeric tea contains a high amount of antioxidants, including vitamin C and E, which help protect the gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, reducing swelling and promoting healing.
Cicatrizant properties: Turmeric's cicatrizant properties help to stimulate collagen synthesis, which promotes wound healing and reduces gum swelling by promoting new tissue growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce gum swelling.
Rhyzomes: Turmeric's rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat gum disease and inflammation due to their ability to reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of fresh turmeric root or half teaspoon dried turmeric powder in two cups of water for ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and pulp.
Add honey or other sweetener to taste as desired taking care not to overpower the turmeric flavor.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional five minutes before serving hot or chilled according to personal preference.
Drink one cup of the turmeric tea twice daily for three weeks or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with gum swelling because its active compound, menthol, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and discomfort. The cooling sensation of peppermint also increases blood flow to the affected area, helping to alleviate swollen gums caused by gingivitis or other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of peppermint may help combat bacteria that contribute to gum swelling. By drinking herbal peppermint tea regularly, individuals can potentially experience relief from gum swelling and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial compounds: Peppermint tea contains antibacterial compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which help combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in peppermint tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling in the gums by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are pro-inflammatory hormones.

Mouthwash-like effect: Peppermint tea's menthol content can help numb the gum tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums, while also stimulating saliva production to wash away bacteria and debris.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are rich in menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with gum swelling.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain compounds that help to reduce gum inflammation and alleviate symptoms of swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried peppermint leaves and measure out two teaspoons for every eight ounces of water.
Rinse the peppermint leaves with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and add them to a teapot or infuser.
Heat distilled water in a kettle or pot to a rolling boil for one minute before reducing heat to a simmer.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for five to seven minutes, depending on desired tea strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving hot or iced as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with gum swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the gums. The tea's flavonoids and terpenes work together to soothe and calm irritated gum tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of lavender tea help to combat gum infections that can cause swelling, promoting healthy oral hygiene and a reduced risk of periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: English lavender tea contains antioxidants like flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative damage in the gums, thereby reducing swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to gum swelling.

Calming and soothing properties: Lavender's calming and soothing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are known triggers for gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a more relaxed mouth environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums.
Buds: The buds of the lavender plant contain flavonoids, which have antibacterial properties that can help combat infections causing gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried english lavender flowers in amounts of one teaspoon per cup.
Heat filtered water to 212°f and let it cool down for exactly five minutes.
Add the harvested dried lavender flowers to the cooled water and steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining flower particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to drinking temperature before consuming one cup two times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves teas helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce redness and discomfort. The compounds found in cloves, such as eugenol, have natural analgesic and antiseptic qualities that help soothe the gums and underlying tissues. As a result, herbal cloves teas can provide relief from gum inflammation and swelling caused by various factors, including gingivitis, dental work, or even hormonal changes during pregnancy or menstruation. This natural remedy promotes healthy gums and overall oral well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cloves contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum swelling.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compound eugenol in cloves has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Antimicrobial properties: Clove tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria that cause gum inflammation and swelling, reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of cloves contain a high concentration of eugenol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce gum swelling.
Roots: Clove roots have been traditionally used to treat oral health issues, including gum inflammation and pain, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried syzygium aromaticum cloves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the cloves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, releasing the aromatic oils from the clove buds.
Strain the tea into another cup using a tea infuser or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Enjoy the warm tea within 20-30 minutes of preparation, sipping it slowly to soothe swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with gum swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and discomfort in the gums. The tea's anethole compound inhibits the production of prostaglandins, responsible for causing inflammation. Additionally, fennel's flavonoids and saponins help to soothe and calm the gum tissue, reducing swelling and pain associated with conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis. As a result, regular consumption of fennel tea can promote healthy gums and prevent further inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tea contains flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in fennel tea, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing.

Mucilage content: Fennel tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and coat the gums, reducing swelling and discomfort by forming a physical barrier against irritants and bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them effective in soothing swollen gums.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have a natural anti-inflammatory property that helps reduce gum swelling and discomfort when consumed as a medicinal tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried fennel leaves and flowers from the foeniculum vulgare plant in sufficient quantities for steeping.
Combine one teaspoon of dried fennel leaves and flowers or two teaspoons of fresh fennel leaves and flowers with 250ml boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the active compounds to dissolve and infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Enjoy the herbal fennel tea hot or warm within 30 minutes of preparation and repeat as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness in the affected area. The tea's flavonoids and carotenoids work together to combat inflammation, soothing irritated gums and promoting a healthy healing process. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial agents help to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum swelling, creating a dual-action solution for alleviating discomfort and promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate swollen gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to swelling, promoting overall oral health.

Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, helping to reduce swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory compounds in roots help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Flowers: Antioxidant-rich flowers contain flavonoids that soothe gum irritation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers fresh from your garden or store them in an airtight container for up to one year.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried calendula flowers or four to six fresh petals and place them in a teapot.
Pour boiling water over the calendula flowers and let it steep for five to seven minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herb residue and discard the solids.
Drink the warm herbal tea three times daily as needed and enjoy the benefits of reduced gum swelling within weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with gum swelling because its active compounds, such as acetanilide and coumarin, possess anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce inflammation in the gums. The tea's antiseptic and antimicrobial actions also help combat oral bacteria, preventing further irritation and infection. Additionally, yarrow's astringent properties can help constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with gum inflammation. This natural remedy provides a gentle and soothing approach to alleviate gum swelling, promoting a healthier and more comfortable mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like acetanaptaen, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, alleviating symptoms of periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in yarrow tea possess antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections and swelling.

Vasoconstrictive effects: Yarrow's volatile oils, such as chamazulene, have vasoconstrictive effects that help reduce blood flow to the affected area, thereby decreasing swelling and alleviating gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain a compound called sesquiterpene lactone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling.
Flowers: The flowers of the yarrow plant have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Rinse one tablespoon of yarrow herb with boiling water to ensure cleanliness.
Steep the yarrow herb in one cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids immediately.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before consuming it within 30 minutes of preparation.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum swelling are a natural remedy that can provide relief from swollen, inflamed gums. These teas work by reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria that cause gum disease. Examples of herbal teas that help with gum swelling include peppermint, sage, and chamomile tea, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gums and promote healing. Drinking these teas regularly can improve oral health by reducing gum pain and discomfort, allowing individuals to eat and speak without hesitation, ultimately enhancing their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum swelling with herbal teas is by combining cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger tea, rich in antioxidants, helps reduce inflammation and soothes swollen gums. Peppermint tea's menthol content cools the area, while chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory compounds calm irritated tissues. Drinking 1-2 cups of these teas three times a day can provide relief from gum swelling.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal teas are conditions such as swollen tonsils, sore throats, and mouth ulcers. Herbs like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated mucous membranes, reducing swelling and discomfort. Other herbs like sage, peppermint, and chamomile can also provide relief from gum swelling-related pain and inflammation when consumed as teas or infusions.
